signal: AMOC freshwater flux anomaly enters a third consecutive week — the strongest sustained early-warning signal since the FovS proxy metric was established — while Thwaites glacier grounding line retreat exceeds IPCC upper projections and Amazon vegetation stress expands to 450,000 km².

delta_strip.title: AMOC FovS — Third consecutive anomaly week
delta_strip.one_line: Collapse probability ensemble revised to 34–62% before 2100 vs IPCC AR6 baseline of 10–25%

delta_strip.title: Thwaites grounding line — exceeds IPCC upper projection
delta_strip.one_line: 1.4 km/year retreat; 3.3 m SLR equivalent; marine ice cliff instability dynamics observed

delta_strip.title: Amazon vegetation stress — 450,000 km² below baseline
delta_strip.one_line: 19% deforestation; approaching lower bound of carbon sink–to–emitter threshold

delta_strip.title: Sahel water-food-displacement cascade — F1 confirmed
delta_strip.one_line: 27 million in IPC Phase 3+; Lake Chad aquifer depleting; reverse cascade from Sudan/Mali conflict confirmed

delta_strip.title: Loss & Damage Finance — $0 disbursed of $661M committed
delta_strip.one_line: 100% disbursement gap in first compliance cycle; F2 Regulatory Vacuum confirmed

delta_strip.title: No material developments
delta_strip.one_line: Environmental risks remain the dominant long-term threat across all nine planetary boundaries, with six boundaries transgressed and two in high-risk status. Short-term risk perception has shifted towa
